critic_raw: The design uses 'discharge disposition' as an admission-level feature. Discharge disposition (e.g., home, skilled nursing facility, hospice) is assigned at the end of the admission — it is a post-admission outcome that would not be available at the beginning of the admission or during the hospital stay. If the model uses discharge disposition as a feature to predict 30-day readmission, it would be using information available only after the outcome is partially determined (a patient discharged to hospice is less likely to be readmitted). This is potential temporal leakage.
defender_raw: Discharge disposition is available at the time of discharge — the model predicts 30-day readmission from discharge, not from admission. Discharge is the prediction point: at the moment a patient is discharged, all admission-level features including discharge disposition are known. The design's feature set is 'computed from information available at the time of diagnosis' — for discharge-based prediction, discharge disposition is known at the prediction time. The design is predicting post-discharge readmission, so discharge disposition is an appropriate feature. This is the standard feature set for 30-day readmission models (see LACE index which includes 'Acuity' based on discharge characteristics).
adjudication_raw: One concern: discharge disposition as temporal leakage. Resolved: prediction point is at discharge, not admission; discharge disposition is available at prediction time; this is standard for 30-day readmission models. Verdict: defense_wins.
